Both files are clean. The PRP is fully implemented and validated.

## Summary

Implemented **P1.M6.T2.S1** — the SIGINT/SIGTERM → cancel-and-rescue signal handler for the atomic-commit pipeline.

### Files created

**`internal/generate/signal.go`** (stdlib + `internal/ui` only, plain `package generate` line — `generate.go` owns the package doc per `rescue.go`/`dedupe.go` convention):
- `var exit = os.Exit` — injectable exit seam
- `var commitSignals = []os.Signal{syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM}` — frozen set
- `var activeCh chan os.Signal` — one-active-handler tracker
- `installSignalHandler(ctxCancel, rescueFn func())` — buffered(1) `signal.Notify` + goroutine that calls `exit(handleSignal(...))`; armed post-snapshot (after `WriteTree`, before generation) per PRD §18.4
- `handleSignal(ctxCancel, rescueFn func()) int` — **pure, os.Exit-free core** with nil-guards on both args; returns `ui.ExitRescue` (the constant, not `3`)
- `restoreSignalHandler()` — calls **both** `signal.Stop(activeCh)` and `signal.Reset(commitSignals...)`; documented as called immediately **BEFORE** `git.UpdateRefCAS` (Gotcha #1)
- Documented the exact 2-call-site CommitStaged wiring snippet for P1.M6.T1.S1 (since `generate.go` doesn't exist yet — scope boundary respected, no stub created)

**`internal/generate/signal_test.go`** (white-box `package generate`; reuses `containsAll` from `rescue_test.go`):
- Pure unit tests: cancel→rescue→`ExitRescue` ordering, `handleSignal(nil,nil)` no-panic, partial-nil guards, restore clears `activeCh`
- Subprocess re-exec integration tests via `STAGEHAND_SIGNAL_TEST` env (parent/child `READY` handshake over stdout for flake-free timing):
  - `TestSignal_RescueOnSIGINT` — real SIGINT → §18.3 rescue block with frozen `TREE_SHA` + exit `3`
  - `TestSignal_RestoreBeforeUpdateRef` — after restore, SIGINT during mock update-ref → **no** rescue block + killed by SIGINT (exit 130, ≠ 3)

### Validation gates (all pass)
- L1: `go build`, `go vet`, `gofmt` clean ✓
- L2: `go test -run HandleSignal` ✓
- L3: `go test -run Signal` (incl. subprocess) ✓ — verified non-flaky over 5 runs
- L4: `go test ./internal/generate/` full package (rescue/dedupe unaffected) ✓
- Full module `go build ./...` ✓; LSP clean ✓

```json

   "result": "success",
   "message": "Implemented internal/generate/signal.go with installSignalHandler/restoreSignalHandler + pure handleSignal (nil-guarded, returns ui.ExitRescue via an injectable exit seam) and internal/generate/signal_test.go (pure unit tests + subprocess re-exec integration tests). restoreSignalHandler uses BOTH signal.Stop and signal.Reset. All six validation gates pass: go build, go vet, gofmt clean, go test -run HandleSignal, go test -run Signal (real SIGINT → rescue block with frozen TREE_SHA + exit 3; post-restore SIGINT → no rescue + default disposition 130), and full package go test ./internal/generate/ (no regression to rescue/dedupe). Scope respected: generate.go does not exist, so no CommitStaged stub was created — the exact two-call-site wiring is documented in a code comment for P1.M6.T1.S1. No testify/external deps; stdlib + internal/ui only; plain package generate line."
}
```
